description: Nintendo and Intelligent Systems' tactical RPG series. Turn-based strategy with permanent character death (permadeath). Spans NES through modern platforms, with the GBA era as the western entry point.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Shadow Dragon and the Blade of Light
media.description: Marth's first campaign to reclaim his kingdom from the Shadow Dragon Medeus. JP only; official English release arrived in 2020.
media.notes: Core: fceumm. Use fan patch or the official 2020 limited release.

media.title: Fire Emblem Gaiden
media.description: Alm and Celica's dual campaigns on the continent of Valentia. Unique mechanics — magic costs HP, no weapon triangle. Remade as Echoes (3DS).
media.notes: Core: fceumm. Fan translation exists. Superseded by Echoes if you have 3DS access.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Mystery of the Emblem
media.description: Compilation sequel — Book 1 remakes Shadow Dragon, Book 2 continues Marth's story. The best version of Marth's arc before the DS remake.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Genealogy of the Holy War
media.description: Sigurd and Seliph's multi-generational saga across two campaigns. Epic scope, deeply political story. Considered by many to be the best in the series.
media.notes: Core: snes9x. Fan translation considered near-official quality — see fireemblemwiki.org.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Thracia 776
media.description: Leif's campaign set during the middle of Genealogy of the Holy War. Notoriously difficult. For series veterans only.
media.notes: Core: snes9x. Use the 'lil' manster' fan translation patch.

media.title: Fire Emblem: The Binding Blade
media.description: Roy's game — reclaims Elibe from the Dragon King. Takes place after Blazing Blade chronologically. Play Blazing Blade first.
media.notes: Core: mgba. Fan translation from romhacking.net.

media.title: Fire Emblem (Blazing Blade)
media.description: Lyn, Eliwood, and Hector unite to stop a scheming dark druid. The first western release and the best entry point for newcomers.
media.notes: Core: mgba. Save ID: fe-blazing. Officially titled just 'Fire Emblem' in western release.

media.title: Fire Emblem: The Sacred Stones
media.description: Eirika and Ephraim defend their kingdom from a demon king. More accessible than Blazing Blade, with a world map and optional grinding.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Path of Radiance
media.description: Ike leads a mercenary band across a continent torn by racial strife between laguz and beorc. Play before Radiant Dawn.
media.notes: Dolphin + Sunshine streaming required. See GameCube tab for setup.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Radiant Dawn
media.description: Direct sequel to Path of Radiance. Micaiah leads a liberation army while Ike returns. Play Path of Radiance first — Radiant Dawn spoils it constantly.
media.notes: Dolphin + Sunshine streaming required. Import save from Path of Radiance for bonuses.

media.title: Fire Emblem: Shadow Dragon
media.description: DS remake of the original NES game. Marth's campaign rebuilt with modern mechanics. Stripped down relative to Mystery of the Emblem.

media.title: Fire Emblem: New Mystery of the Emblem
media.description: DS remake of Mystery of the Emblem Book 2, with a customizable avatar unit. JP only — never localized.
media.notes: Core: melonds. Use Serenes Forest community fan translation patch.

media.title: Fire Emblem Awakening
media.description: Chrom and a customizable avatar face a resurrected fell dragon. Introduced pair-up mechanics and children characters. Saved the franchise commercially.
media.notes: 3DS — requires Citra/Lime3DS/Azahar emulator. Not browser-playable. Sunshine streaming needed.

media.title: Fire Emblem Fates
media.description: Three-route game (Birthright / Conquest / Revelation) where the avatar must choose between two kingdoms. Conquest has the best gameplay; Birthright is most accessible.
media.notes: 3DS — requires Citra/Lime3DS/Azahar emulator. Three separate ROMs. Sunshine streaming needed.
